75611.subject: Traders Sue Polymarket Over Its Strategy Bitcoin Market
75611.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

These were the biggest news in web3 today:

* **[Polymarket](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/traders-sue-polymarket-ceo-coplan-over-strategy-market-decision)**[ sued over Strategy market resolution](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/traders-sue-polymarket-ceo-coplan-over-strategy-market-decision)

* **[KAST](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/kast-s-terms-of-service-draw-fire-after-public-feud-with-etherfi-ceo)**['s terms of service draw fire](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/kast-s-terms-of-service-draw-fire-after-public-feud-with-etherfi-ceo)

* **[Tether](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/tether-invests-20m-in-brazil-s-mercado-bitcoin)**[ invests $20M in Mercado Bitcoin](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/tether-invests-20m-in-brazil-s-mercado-bitcoin)

* **[BonkDAO's attacker](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/bonkdao-attacker-moves-19m-loot-into-new-bonk-2-0-dao)**[ moves loot ](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/bonkdao-attacker-moves-19m-loot-into-new-bonk-2-0-dao)

* **[CAP](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/cap-token-climbs-to-2-lending-borrowing-protocol-by-volume-10-days-after-launch)**[ climbs to the #2 lending protocol by volume](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/cap-token-climbs-to-2-lending-borrowing-protocol-by-volume-10-days-after-launch)

* **[MGUSD, Explained: How MoneyGram Built a Stablecoin](https://thedefiant.io/education/explainers/mgusd-moneygram-stablecoin-stellar-distribution-explainer)**[ (Partner)](https://thedefiant.io/education/explainers/mgusd-moneygram-stablecoin-stellar-distribution-explainer)

The week's biggest Strategy question — would Michael Saylor sell bitcoin — was a controversial market on Polymarket. Now two traders are suing over how that market was called. Two Polymarket users [sued the platform and CEO Shayne Coplan](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/traders-sue-polymarket-ceo-coplan-over-strategy-market-decision) in New York state court, alleging the site **wrongly resolved a market on whether Strategy would sell Bitcoin**. The complaint from William Wood and Thomas Bush lands the same week Strategy [actually sold 3,588 BTC](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/strategy-sells-3-588-bitcoin-for-216m-to-fund-dividend-payments) — turning a contested resolution into a live dispute over what "sell" meant and when it counted.

The suit puts the softest part of prediction markets on trial: resolution. Kalshi and Polymarket have raised at multibillion-dollar valuations on the promise that outcomes are objective, but someone still has to decide when a market pays. A courtroom fight over a single Strategy contract is really a fight over who that someone is — and whether traders can sue when they disagree.

**Read more below!**

———————————————————————————

##### PARTNER

Caption:

75609.subject: 🟪 Shakespeare's dollar
75609.body:

Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

GONZALO: When every grief is entertained that’s offered, comes to th’ entertainer—

SEBASTIAN: A dollar.

_— _William Shakespeare, _The Tempest_



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/61e1c711-a565-4b9f-a5d3-08bd8d095bf3/image.jpeg?t=1783440153)
Caption: 

# Shakespeare's dollar

In the 250th anniversary-year of America, Brendan Greeley writes the 500-year history of its currency.

“The dollar,” he [says](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=the+almighty+dollar&crid=3Q6H6X1WNI2Q6&sprefix=the+almighty%2Caps%2C237&ref=nb_sb_ss_p13n-expert-pd-ops-ranker_2_12), “is both older and more powerful than the United States.”

The story begins in the silver mines of Joachim’s Valley in modern-day Czechia, where Joachimsthaler were first minted in 1520.

The big silver coins — known as _thaler_ (rhymes with dollar) — weren’t meant to be money. They seemed too valuable for that. Instead, they were minted as dividends to pay out to the investors who funded the mine.

Paying investors in ingots (too irregular) or bars (too large) was inconvenient. Sending the silver to the regional Bohenmian mint was inconvenient and expensive, cutting into the mine’s already razor-thin profits.

To avoid all that, the operator of the mine — Count Stephan Schlick — built his own mint, striking coins with an image of St. Joachim on the front and the Lion of Bohemia on the back. At 29.2 grams of nearly pure silver, the coins were far too valuable to pay miners' wages, but ideal for paying investors.

As investors collected their dividends, however, the coins inevitably began circulating as money — _big_ money, used to settle the Early Modern economy’s biggest transactions. 

Prized for their purity, standardization, and trustworthiness, thaler became a currency of international trade: “a way to move a lot of value over long distances,” Greeley explains. 

Soon, they were the standard, with mints around Europe compelled to copy them because merchants and bankers wanted nothing else. Close replicas of the thaler included the Danish rigsdaler, Dutch rijksdaalder_, _Habsburg doler_,_ and Swedish riksdaler.

All of which rhymed with dollar. In the 16th century, a “dollar” referred to the original thaler or any of its ne

75608.subject: ‘I’m Now Broke’: These Investors Lost Billions On Trump Stocks And Crypto
75608.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/im-now-broke-investors-lost-billions-trump-stocks-crypto-wprlc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BpEK9qaWrR5uFpWc7X5tPRQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3RNHba4NqEzIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrb5kduq~en-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrb5kduq-en
  
Good evening, In October 2021, Donald Trump unveiled Truth Social, promising a censorship-free platform and offering supporters a chance to invest via a special purpose acquisition company. Shares of the SPAC jumped 1,650% in two days, then fell about 30% by the start of the third. However, the believers didn’t quite see the payday they expected. “Donald Trump is a b----,” Chad Nedohin, a worship leader who once moonlighted as the unofficial captain of Trump Media’s retail shareholders, said on the matter. Read more here. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images How OpenAI Plans To Win Over Doctors, Patients And Hospitals The AI juggernaut is all-in on the healthcare industry—but it needs to ensure ChatGPT is accurate enough for the millions who already use it for health advice. Illustration by Macy Sinreich for Forbes; Images by fhm via Getty Images, Penpak S via Getty Images AI Data Centers Are Set To Electrify This $13 Billion Family’s 76-Year-Old Company The company has made a bundle for the Richards family, who still owns 100% of the business and is worth approximately $13.1 billion, Forbes estimates. The family fortune will likely continue to grow due to the explosion of AI data centers.
Приєднатися до обговорення
